patch.value: Stabilize npx onboarding path for scoped pm package

Fix GitHub issue #131 by making the first-command package execution path obvious and regression-tested for agents. Scope includes canonical npx docs, package/release smoke coverage, dependency/security triage, changelog validation, and temp-project dogfood.

value.author: package-lane-codex
value.text: Subagent package/npm lane: verified npm 11.12.1 / node v25.9.0 behavior for GitHub issue #131. Published @unbrained/pm-cli@latest metadata is name=@unbrained/pm-cli version=2026.6.6 bin={pm:dist/cli.js, pm-mcp:dist/mcp/server.js}. Repro: npx --yes @unbrained/pm-cli@latest --help fails with 'npm error could not determine executable to run'. Control: npx --yes --package @unbrained/pm-cli@latest -- pm --help succeeds. Cause: bare npx infers the executable from the unscoped package name pm-cli when multiple bins exist; current metadata has no pm-cli bin, so npm cannot choose. Package.json can make the exact scoped command work without changing the package name or removing bins by adding a bin alias: pm-cli -> dist/cli.js while keeping pm and pm-mcp. A temporary packed package with that alias made npx --yes file:<tarball> --help render pm help and --package <tarball> pm-cli --version return 2026.6.6. Smallest durable implementation/test set: add package.json bin.pm-cli, update scripts/smoke-npx-from-pack.mjs to use file:<tarballPath> for bare npx package-spec checks and assert both bare npx help/version plus explicit --package pm and pm-cli alias, and update scripts/release/verify-published-release.mjs to verify direct npx plus explicit --package pm after publish. Docs: README.md and docs/QUICKSTART.md should advertise npx --yes @unbrained/pm-cli@latest --help; MCP/plugin docs should keep using npx --yes --package=@unbrained/pm-cli@latest pm-mcp because direct npx runs the CLI alias, not pm-mcp. Validation run: npm install -g . passed; pnpm build passed; npx published repro/control passed. Current concurrent worktree note: package.json already contains pm-cli alias, but pnpm smoke:npx currently fails on npm 11 because direct checks use raw tarballPath, producing sh: <tarball>.tgz: Permission denied; change those direct npx invocations to file:<tarballPath>. I made no repository file edits in this lane.

value.author: security-lane-codex
value.text: Subagent GitHub/security lane: bootstrap OK (npm install -g ., pm 2026.6.6, node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.4, pnpm build passed). gh auth OK for unbraind/pm-cli. GitHub live state: no open PRs; one open issue #131 matching this item; latest main push runs green for Docs 27075523868, CodeQL 27075523866, CI 27075523863; recent stale PR-branch failures exist but were superseded by green reruns/merged main. GitHub alert surfaces: open Dependabot alerts [], code scanning alerts [], secret scanning alerts []. Local security: pnpm audit --audit-level moderate passed with no known vulnerabilities; pnpm security:scan passed with no credential-like tracked secrets. Dependency drift: pnpm outdated --format json reports only commander current/wanted 14.0.3, latest 15.0.0. Release/telemetry: direct sentry-telemetry gate passed using SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN, 0 critical/high Sentry issues, telemetry finish error rate 1.9%, 0 missing error-code rows; not blocked by missing secrets. Broader pnpm release:gates failed during coverage on tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts keeps update-close workflow behavior deterministic (createResult.code 1), but exact focused rerun passed via node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts -t 'keeps update-close workflow behavior deterministic'; classify as non-reproduced coverage-run failure for main lane triage.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Evidence: implemented GitHub issue #131 by adding package.json bin alias pm-cli -> dist/cli.js while preserving pm and pm-mcp. Updated packed npx smoke to pack into a temp root, verify direct package-spec execution with file:<tarball>, verify pm-cli alias help/version, and retain package-first install/catalog/calendar/upgrade smoke. Updated post-publish verifier to check direct npx plus explicit --package pm. Updated README/Quickstart direct CLI npx examples and plugin MCP docs to use explicit --package pm-mcp fallback. Subagents recorded package/npm and GitHub/security/Sentry findings on this item.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Verification: pnpm build passed; pnpm smoke:npx passed (packed package smoke, packages=9); manual temp-dir npx file:<tarball> dogfood passed by init/create/list-open with created item pm-hsd5; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts passed (33 tests); node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed (146 files, 2528 tests, 100% statements/branches/functions/lines); pnpm quality:docs-links passed; pnpm quality:static passed; pnpm security:scan passed; pnpm audit --audit-level moderate passed; pnpm changelog:pm:check passed unchanged with pm-changelog 2026.6.6; pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift passed history_drift ok with pre-existing resolution warnings; strict sentry-telemetry gate passed with SENTRY_PERSONAL_ADMIN_TOKEN, 0 critical/high, telemetry error rate 1.9%.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Scoped npm packages with multiple bins need a bin matching the unscoped package name for bare npx package execution. For @unbrained/pm-cli, adding pm-cli as an alias to dist/cli.js lets npm infer the executable while keeping pm and pm-mcp stable. MCP fallbacks should continue to use --package so they can select pm-mcp explicitly.

Changed packed npx help smoke assertions from fixed copy substrings to non-empty successful help output, grouped published npx verifier variants under result.package.npx with a shared required-executor helper, and corrected plugins/pm-claude README session-hook text to describe the actual pm context CLI invocation rather than pm-mcp. Verification after follow-up: pnpm smoke:npx passed; pnpm quality:docs-links passed; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/integration/release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts passed.
